title: The One with Unagi

jokes.text: Remember when I had a monkey? What was I thinking?
jokes.setup: Ross randomly brings up having a monkey
jokes.punchline: Self-aware criticism of absurd past decision
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that works through self-awareness. Economical but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: 20 percent is a pretty generous tip. You know what's more generous? 50 percent!
jokes.setup: Customer questions 20% tip appropriateness
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurdly high percentage
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke. Works but predictable structure.

jokes.text: You know what's even more generous? I see where you're going.
jokes.setup: Setting up another escalation
jokes.punchline: Character cuts off the pattern before it gets absurd
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of expected escalation. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: I'm down to, like, three. Actually, two, because one of them I blackened some teeth. Why'd I do that?
jokes.setup: Joey needs headshots, has very few left
jokes.punchline: Reveals he vandalized his own headshot for no reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - doing something stupid and being confused by his own actions. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: It helps if you've got a little of this going on.
jokes.setup: Discussing begging for money
jokes.punchline: Character demonstrates pathetic begging posture/expression
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about looking pathetic. Mildly funny but not original.

jokes.text: Wow! I still have it.
jokes.setup: After demonstrating pathetic begging look
jokes.punchline: Character is proud of ability to look pathetic
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to previous joke. Character being proud of pathetic skill is amusing.

jokes.text: Finally, an explanation.
jokes.setup: Joey mentions medical experiments
jokes.punchline: Deadpan suggestion this explains Joey's oddness
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan callback to Joey's general weirdness. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: I really prefer doing that at the home office.
jokes.setup: Joey mentions not wanting to donate sperm again
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to masturbating at home
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism that sounds professional. Classic Joey mixing crude with innocent phrasing.

jokes.text: Do it?
jokes.setup: Boss asks what Joey was told about talking while working
jokes.punchline: Joey completely misunderstood the instruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey stupidity in one word. Maximum economy with strong character integration.

jokes.text: Kicking a guy in the crotch all morning really takes it out of you.
jokes.setup: Explaining they went to self-defense class
jokes.punchline: Phrasing suggests they're the ones who are tired from attacking
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of expectation - they're tired from being the attackers, not victims.

jokes.text: Takes it out of you?
jokes.setup: Previous line about being tired from kicking crotches
jokes.punchline: Ross's incredulous reaction to the phrasing
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat. Ross being the straight man to absurd phrasing.

jokes.text: Isn't that a kind of sushi?
jokes.setup: Ross introduces mystical concept of 'unagi'
jokes.punchline: Character immediately identifies it as food
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Ross's pretentious martial arts moment. Sets up the entire episode's running gag.

jokes.text: Yeah, it is. It's freshwater eel.
jokes.setup: Previous character said unagi is sushi
jokes.punchline: Confidently providing specific food knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of food knowledge, further deflating Ross's mystical concept.

jokes.text: I would kill for a salmon-skin roll right now.
jokes.setup: Discussing sushi after Ross's martial arts lecture
jokes.punchline: Completely abandoning martial arts topic for food craving
jokes.explanation: Nice derailment of serious conversation into food craving. Typical Friends tangent humor.

jokes.text: In case someone's trying to steal your bamboo sleeping mat... or your kettle of fish?
jokes.setup: Ross explains unagi protects from danger
jokes.punchline: Mocking with stereotypical Japanese items
jokes.explanation: Mocking Ross's pseudo-mystical martial arts with Asian stereotypes. Somewhat dated humor.

jokes.text: Surprise! [Ross attacking the women]
jokes.setup: Ross lecturing about being prepared for surprise attacks
jokes.punchline: Ross physically demonstrating by attacking them
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross taking things too far. Physical comedy that's both awkward and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: If we made reservations, we could have unagi in a half-hour.
jokes.setup: Ross continues martial arts lecture about unagi
jokes.punchline: Returning to food interpretation after his demonstration
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that deflates Ross's entire demonstration. Great timing and economy.

jokes.text: Valentine's Day was two weeks ago, so I wouldn't get her a calendar.
jokes.setup: Someone asks for Valentine's Day gift help
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion about getting a calendar
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm. Perfect character voice and economical delivery.

jokes.text: And, of course, crotchless panties.
jokes.setup: Discussing romantic spa day suggestions
jokes.punchline: Joey adds inappropriate sexual suggestion
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey sexual non-sequitur. In character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: As appealing as that sounds to her boyfriend... and her brother...
jokes.setup: Joey suggests crotchless panties for Monica
jokes.punchline: Ross awkwardly noting he's both boyfriend and brother
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awkward acknowledgment of the uncomfortable dynamic. Perfect cringe comedy with the pause.

jokes.text: You can't make crotchless panties? You take scissors and cut..
jokes.setup: Discussion about making gifts vs buying them
jokes.punchline: Joey offers practical DIY advice for inappropriate gift
jokes.explanation: Joey being helpful in completely inappropriate way. Character-consistent but crude.

jokes.text: I thought so too, until I papier-mached one of my eyes shut.
jokes.setup: Someone says making things sounds fun
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's craft project disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe mishap - specific, absurd, and completely believable for her character.

jokes.text: I made a flar-lar-lar.
jokes.setup: Asked what she made with papier-mache
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's made-up word for her creation
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Phoebe nonsense word. Highly quotable and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Oh, for crying out loud!
jokes.setup: Ross mentions Phoebe making sock bunnies
jokes.punchline: Monica's exasperated reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction. In character but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. That is my sock!
jokes.setup: Ross showing off sock bunny Phoebe made
jokes.punchline: Monica realizes Phoebe stole her sock
jokes.explanation: Good escalation reveal. Monica's possessiveness coming through.

jokes.text: Now it's your little bunny friend.
jokes.setup: Monica upset about sock theft
jokes.punchline: Phoebe casually reframing theft as gift-giving
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe deflection. Sweet tone masking the theft with innocent reframing.

jokes.text: This actually is a flar-lar-lar.
jokes.setup: Earlier established flar-lar-lar as nonsense word
jokes.punchline: Phoebe revealing her creation actually matches the nonsense name
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes the nonsense word somehow make sense in Phoebe's world.

jokes.text: I can't believe it! Make the presents!
jokes.setup: Chandler realizes he forgot to make Monica a gift
jokes.punchline: Trying to cover panic with fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Standard panic recovery attempt. Chandler's awkwardness showing through.

jokes.text: Just so excited to make the presents!
jokes.setup: Continuing to cover up his panic
jokes.punchline: Obvious overcompensation with fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Continuation of previous joke. Diminishing returns on the panic cover-up.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Joey Tribbiani. With all due respect, I'd like to donate some fluids.
jokes.setup: Joey entering medical research facility
jokes.punchline: Overly formal introduction for crude request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey mix of trying to be polite while being crude. The formal 'with all due respect' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That's too bad. I've been saving up.
jokes.setup: Told they're not accepting applicants
jokes.punchline: Joey implies he's been storing bodily fluids
jokes.explanation: Gross but economical. Joey's innocent delivery makes the innuendo funnier.

jokes.text: Testing the Effects of Joey Tribbiani on Attractive Nurse Receptionist.
jokes.setup: Joey reading study titles
jokes.punchline: Joey makes up study about his own attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey ego moment. Making up a scientific study about his own appeal is exactly what he'd do.

jokes.text: Results are back on that one. They're not good.
jokes.setup: Joey's made-up study about his attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Receptionist plays along with devastating results
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback that deflates Joey's ego perfectly. Great straight-man delivery.

jokes.text: What the hell was that?
jokes.setup: Ross just surprised attacked them making siren sounds
jokes.punchline: Incredulous reaction to Ross's bizarre behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard shocked reaction. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: A lesson in the importance of unagi.
jokes.setup: Asked to explain his bizarre attack
jokes.punchline: Ross treating his assault as educational
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ross being pretentious about his inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: At what point during those girlish screams would you have kicked my ass?
jokes.setup: Women claim they could kick his ass
jokes.punchline: Ross mocking their reaction to his surprise attack
jokes.explanation: Ross being obnoxiously smug after his surprise attack. Good escalation of his arrogance.

jokes.text: Danger!
jokes.setup: Ross lecturing them about awareness
jokes.punchline: They surprise attack Ross while he's being smug
jokes.explanation: Perfect turnabout. Ross gets a taste of his own medicine with great timing.

jokes.text: Ah-ha! Unagi!
jokes.setup: After attacking Ross
jokes.punchline: Using his own mystical concept against him
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that turns Ross's pretentious concept against him. Great payoff.

jokes.text: 2000 bucks is a lot of money. I wish I had a twin. Where could I find someone... who looks exactly like me?
jokes.setup: Joey wants money from twin study
jokes.punchline: Not realizing the logical impossibility of finding his identical twin
jokes.explanation: Good setup for upcoming Carl storyline. Joey's obliviousness to basic genetics is perfect.

jokes.text: Chandler. I sensed it was you.
jokes.setup: Chandler entering apartment
jokes.punchline: Ross claiming mystical awareness of obvious arrival
jokes.explanation: Ross continuing his pretentious martial arts phase. Mildly amusing character consistency.

jokes.text: Are you aware that unagi is an eel?
jokes.setup: Ross mentions unagi mysticism
jokes.punchline: Chandler deflating with food fact
jokes.explanation: Good callback delivered in Chandler's deadpan style. Continues deflating Ross's pretensions.

jokes.text: It's a replica of Apollo 8. I made it in the sixth grade.
jokes.setup: Ross offering homemade item to help Chandler
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's a childhood school project
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ross being nerdy enough to keep childhood space projects.

jokes.text: I could use that. I could say, 'Your love sends me to the moon.'
jokes.setup: Seeing Ross's Apollo 8 model
jokes.punchline: Chandler's cheesy romantic interpretation
jokes.explanation: Chandler being desperate enough to use cliched romantic line. Mildly amusing.

jokes.text: Except Apollo 8 didn't actually land on the moon. You can say, 'Your love lets me orbit the moon twice and return safely.'
jokes.setup: Chandler's moon landing romantic line
jokes.punchline: Ross correcting with overly specific space facts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - pedantically correcting romance with space facts. Excellent character integration and originality.

jokes.text: I can't give this to her.
jokes.setup: After Ross's space fact correction
jokes.punchline: Sets up upcoming revelation about why not
jokes.explanation: Setup line. Functional but not inherently funny.

jokes.text: It says 'Captain Ross' on the side.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he can't give Ross's model to Monica
jokes.punchline: Obvious personalization makes it unusable
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - first obvious problem with using Ross's childhood project as gift.

jokes.text: And it says 'I hate Monica' on the bottom.
jokes.setup: Already revealed 'Captain Ross' makes it unusable
jokes.punchline: Even worse inscription about Monica specifically
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with great timing. Ross hating Monica as kids adds character depth and is very funny.

jokes.text: Danger!
jokes.setup: Another Ross surprise attack
jokes.punchline: Ross continuing his unagi demonstrations
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation. Less funny than first time but maintains character arc.

jokes.text: Salmon-skin roll.
jokes.setup: Ross doing his unagi thing
jokes.punchline: Chandler reducing it to sushi order
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with maximum economy. Two words deflate entire martial arts pretension.

jokes.text: I can't do it. I can't do it.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to lie to Monica about making her gift
jokes.punchline: His inability to maintain the deception
jokes.explanation: Standard panic moment. Shows Chandler's inability to lie but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: A mixed tape! A mixed tape!
jokes.setup: Chandler panicking about gift
jokes.punchline: Last-minute inspiration for simple gift
jokes.explanation: Simple desperation solution. Sets up upcoming complications but not inherently funny.

jokes.text: It's a sock bunny. Remember how I call you bunny? Not really. I did one time. And I want to start doing it more.
jokes.setup: Chandler giving Monica the stolen sock bunny
jokes.punchline: Desperately trying to justify the pet name he never used
jokes.explanation: Good awkward lie that escalates beautifully. Chandler's desperation making him dig deeper.

jokes.text: Phoebe makes sock bunnies. No, she doesn't. What she makes, they're sock rabbits. They're completely different.
jokes.setup: Monica recognizing Phoebe's work
jokes.punchline: Chandler making up meaningless distinction
jokes.explanation: Great desperate distinction. The absurdity of bunny vs rabbit difference is perfect Chandler.

jokes.text: I didn't make it! I forgot about tonight and that we're supposed to make the presents.
jokes.setup: Trying to maintain the lie about the sock bunny
jokes.punchline: Chandler breaks down and confesses everything
jokes.explanation: Payoff to building tension. Chandler's guilt making him confess is in character.

jokes.text: Well, I did put a lot of thought into the tape.
jokes.setup: Monica being very generous about his gift failure
jokes.punchline: Chandler understating his minimal effort
jokes.explanation: Sets up upcoming revelation about the tape. Mild understatement humor.

jokes.text: It's like that old saying, 'Have sex, eat some cake.'
jokes.setup: Monica suggesting activities for the evening
jokes.punchline: Chandler making up a fake old saying
jokes.explanation: Good fake saying that sounds plausible. Chandler's attempt at being smooth.

jokes.text: Who the hell are you? I'm Joey. How are you doing?
jokes.setup: Monica encounters Carl pretending to be Joey
jokes.punchline: Carl doing Joey's signature line badly
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Carl storyline. The bad delivery of Joey's line is appropriately awkward.

jokes.text: No! No! No! No! No! How you doing? How you doing? Damn it, Carl! Go wait in the hall.
jokes.setup: Carl delivering Joey's line wrong
jokes.punchline: Joey desperately trying to coach his fake twin
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Joey's frustration. His desperation to perfect his signature line is very in character.

jokes.text: Sometimes the good ideas are right in front of you.
jokes.setup: Explaining hiring Carl as his twin
jokes.punchline: Joey thinking this obviously bad idea is good
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Joey's 'wisdom' about obviously terrible ideas. Character-appropriate obliviousness.

jokes.text: We were supposed to be brothers, but he messed up. Can I get a piece of that cake? Pizza! We like pizza! Get out!
jokes.setup: Joey explaining Carl's past failure
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating Carl's terrible acting with escalating panic
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Carl's terrible improv skills. The randomness and panic are perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You will not find a single game-show host whose ass I cannot kick.
jokes.setup: Listing various game show hosts
jokes.punchline: Ross's weirdly specific boast about fighting game show hosts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd boast. The specificity of game show hosts makes it hilarious and very quotable.

jokes.text: It's not something you are, it's something you have.
jokes.setup: Women saying they 'are unagi'
jokes.punchline: Ross correcting their grammar about his made-up concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross pedantry - correcting grammar about something he made up. Very in character.

jokes.text: Carl, we're next. Now remember, what is this not gonna be? Another Minute Maid fiasco.
jokes.setup: Joey prepping Carl for medical study
jokes.punchline: Reference to another commercial disaster
jokes.explanation: Good callback establishing Carl's track record. Builds anticipation for upcoming failure.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna talk because.. Damn it, Carl!
jokes.setup: Joey coaching Carl not to talk
jokes.punchline: Carl immediately talking and messing up
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate failure. Carl fails instantly at the one thing he was told not to do.

jokes.text: But you're not identical twins.
jokes.setup: Joey and Carl claiming to be identical twins
jokes.punchline: Researcher stating the obvious about their appearance
jokes.explanation: Simple deflation of Joey's ridiculous plan. Researcher as straight man to obvious deception.

jokes.text: Damn it, Carl!
jokes.setup: Plan failing due to obvious visual differences
jokes.punchline: Joey blaming Carl for unfixable problem
jokes.explanation: Good running gag. Joey blaming Carl for things beyond his control is absurd and funny.

jokes.text: Continuing the countdown of your favorite meals. Tonight, number three: Macaroni and cheese with cut-up hot dogs.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to be romantic
jokes.punchline: Treating childish comfort food like gourmet cuisine
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of romantic presentation with kiddie food. Very sweet and funny.

jokes.text: I love the way you look every night, Chandler.
jokes.setup: Dancing to romantic music
jokes.punchline: Monica's genuine romantic response
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment that sets up upcoming comedy. More about character development than humor.

jokes.text: That's why I made you this tape. Happy birthday! Love, Janice!
jokes.setup: Romantic moment with 'Chandler's' mixtape
jokes.punchline: Revealing the tape is actually from Janice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant revelation that destroys the romantic moment perfectly. Great setup/payoff structure with perfect timing.

jokes.text: I was watching. A couple of questions: You know that last move... where that woman tripped you and pinned you to the floor? What would you do next?
jokes.setup: Instructor addressing Ross after watching him get beaten up
jokes.punchline: Treating his defeat as learning opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Ross's embarrassment. Instructor treating his failure as teachable moment.

jokes.text: I tried attacking two women. Did not work.
jokes.setup: Asked why he wants to learn self-defense
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact admission of failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross understatement. The casual admission of attacking women is both horrible and hilarious.

jokes.text: You attacked your ex-wife? Oh, no. No, no. I tried. But I couldn't. That's why I'm here.
jokes.setup: Instructor's shocked reaction to attacking women
jokes.punchline: Ross making it worse by explaining he 'tried' to attack his ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Ross makes himself sound worse while trying to clarify. Perfect cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Maybe we could attack them together!
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his training needs
jokes.punchline: Professional instructor suggesting they team up to attack women
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - the instructor getting caught up in Ross's absurd logic. Very unexpected.

jokes.text: That's a no.
jokes.setup: Just suggested attacking women together
jokes.punchline: Instructor immediately realizing how inappropriate that was
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment. Simple but effective correction of the absurd suggestion.

jokes.text: Nowhere to run.
jokes.setup: Ross approaching the women in the coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Ross acting like a movie villain stalking prey
jokes.explanation: Good buildup to final unagi demonstration. Ross being creepy in his martial arts obsession.

jokes.text: Danger! Oh, my God! Why is he jumping on those women? We should help them. I don't think they need any help.
jokes.setup: Ross attacking the women again
jokes.punchline: Chandler realizing the women are winning the fight
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Ross's overconfidence. The visual of him getting beaten up by women he was trying to teach is perfect.

tmdb_overview: Joey enters medical study for twins. Rachel and Phoebe take self-defense while Ross teaches 'Unagi.' Chandler and Monica exchange homemade Valentine's gifts.
